Prisma is a modern, next generation ORM for Node.js and TypeScript. It replaces traditional ORMs like Sequelize or TypeORM by focussing on type safety, readability, performance and developer experience.
: The single source of truth (schema.prisma) where you define your database connection, generators, and data models.
: An auto-generated, type-safe query builder that matches your schema exactly, ensuring compile-time errors if a query is malformed.
: A declarative data modeling and migration system that maps your Prisma schema to your database tables.
: A visual GUI built into the toolkit to view, search, and edit data in your database.
Starting point of a Prisma project
// Connect to the database and query data
Difference between Prisma and Traditional ORMs
Fully auto-generated from the schema; guaranteed type-safety.
Often relies on manual classes/decorators; prone to drift.
Uses custom OOP instances, repositories, or raw string SQL.
Defined across multiple model files using classes.
Offloaded to a fast execution engine written in Rust.
Handled completely in the JavaScript/Node.js event loop.
Lazy loading supported, which often causes N+1 query issues.
Very low; feels like writing natural objects.
Moderate to high; requires learning complex ORM syntax.
